title: Lebanon and Israel meet in Washington for tough negotiations under growing pressure
subhead: Direct talks begin Thursday between the Israeli government, which has little incentive to compromise, and Lebanese officials unable to disarm Hezbollah, as deadly escalation continues between the Israeli military and the Shiite movement.

title: Xi warns Trump Taiwan could lead to 'conflict' at Beijing summit
With CMA Média, shipping magnate Rodolphe Saadé expands his empire
Sarkozy appeal trial: Prosecution calls for punishment 'commensurate with the gravity of the offenses'
subhead: The prosecution asked for the same sentence as in the initial trial: seven years in prison for Nicolas Sarkozy, as the 'instigator' and 'sole beneficiary' of a 'corruption pact' with the Libyan regime. Six and four-year sentences were requested for his associates Claude Guéant and Brice Hortefeux.

title: Latvian prime minister resigns over Ukrainian drones straying into Latvia
subhead: Evika Silina resigned after her decision to sack Defense Minister Andris Spruds, over an incident in which Ukrainian drones crashed into Latvian territory led nine MPs to leave her ruling coalition, prompting a no-confidence vote.

title: At the Cannes Film Festival, the art of criticizing without offending: 'It might be your most personal film yet'
subhead: With festival up and running, columnist Guillemette Faure explores the art of expressing an opinion without really stating it, so as not to upset anyone after a screening.

title: How China is cracking down on its Christians
subhead: Le Monde verified footage from Chinese social media and collected testimonies from victims of Beijing's crackdown to reveal how the regime has brought independent churches to heel since Xi Jinping came to power.

'Zinédine Zidane maintains a political and social awareness that is deeply connected to his own story'
subhead: The sociologist Stéphane Beaud explored in an essay the football player's career, framing it within that of the descendants of Algerian immigrants. In an interview, he explains that the athlete tried to make up for the injustices suffered by his father, who remained at the bottom of the social ladder throughout his life.

title: French far-right mayors' first steps target Europe, culture and unions
subhead: Le Monde examined the first decisions made by newly-elected far-right mayors. While they have not yet kept their tax promises, almost all of them have increased their own allowances, hired police officers and targeted local figures who are politically hostile to them.

title: The shattered lives of Iranians Manije, Mohammad-Reza and Soroush, all killed by bombings
subhead: Before the Israeli-US bombings began, they were living as retirees and artists in different parts of Tehran. Until they met their deaths in their own homes. Tormented by distress, their loved ones abroad and in Iran recount their last conversations before fate took a tragic turn.
